# PROJECT EUREKA — IMPLEMENTATION STRATEGY
## AI-Powered Educational SaaS Platform
### Generated by ARCHITECT · Claude Opus 4.6

---

## HOW TO USE THESE FILES

This folder contains two different artifact types:

1. **Prompt specifications** (`01_` through `07_`) — these are the system prompts to store in your backend prompt library and send to the assigned model at runtime.
2. **Architecture documents** (`00_` and `08_`) — these tell you the order in which to build and wire the platform.

Do **not** rely on pasting these prompts manually into consumer chat apps for production use. The production pattern is:
- Store each prompt as a versioned file in the backend
- Load it inside the matching FastAPI service
- Send it as the system/developer instruction when calling the target model API

## BEGINNER-FRIENDLY BUILD APPROACH

If you are new to app development, the biggest mistake is asking an AI model to generate the **entire platform at once**. That usually creates code that looks complete but is hard to run and harder to debug.

Use this approach instead:

1. Ask the AI for **one folder or one file group at a time**
2. Run that code in the correct platform
3. Fix errors immediately
4. Only then ask for the next module
5. Keep all prompts focused on one engine or one infrastructure layer

Your working pattern should be:
- Architecture from these `.md` files
- Code generation from coding models
- Manual verification after every generated module
- Final integration only after each module works independently

## BEST AI TO USE FOR EACH BUILD TASK

| Task Type | Best AI to Generate Code | Why |
|----------|---------------------------|-----|
| FastAPI backend, routers, schemas, services | GPT-5.2 Codex or GPT-5.4 | Best coding reliability for Python app structure and refactors |
| Next.js frontend, components, pages, animations | GPT-5.4 | Strongest for React + Tailwind + Framer Motion + responsive design |
| Design system setup (Tailwind config, shadcn/ui, theme) | GPT-5.4 | Best at generating complete Tailwind configs and component customization |
| Data visualization (Recharts, charts, dashboards) | GPT-5.4 | Strong composable chart generation with animation support |
| Architecture review and prompt improvement | Claude Opus 4.6 | Best for reasoning, reviewing, and refining system behavior |
| Multimodal pipeline design | Gemini 3.1 Pro | Strongest for document/audio/image-oriented workflows |
| Translation workflow design | Gemini 3.1 Pro | Best aligned to multilingual behavior |
| Small utility scripts and boilerplate | GPT-5.1 Codex Mini | Fast and sufficient for low-risk scaffolding |
| Integration testing and E2E test writing | GPT-5.2 Codex | Precise schema-aware test generation |

Recommended beginner tool usage:
- Use **GPT-5.4** to generate all frontend code (Next.js, components, pages, animations)
- Use **GPT-5.2 Codex** to generate all backend code (FastAPI, services, tests)
- Use **Claude Opus 4.6** to review generated code, improve prompts, and design orchestration
- Use **Gemini 3.1 Pro** only for multimodal and translation-specific features
- Build **backend engines first**, then the **web app** as a polished, production-grade UI

## MASTER CODE-GENERATION RULES

Every time you ask an AI to generate code, include these rules:

```text
You are generating production-grade code for Project Eureka.
Generate only the files requested in this step.
Do not invent unrelated modules.
Use clear folder structure.
Return complete file contents.
Add concise comments only where needed.
Include install steps, run steps, env vars, and test steps.
If any assumption is uncertain, state it explicitly.
Keep the code compatible with immediate integration into a FastAPI + Next.js web app.
The web app should use Tailwind CSS 4, shadcn/ui, Framer Motion for animations, and Recharts for data visualization.
The app must be responsive, accessible (WCAG 2.1 AA), and support dark/light mode.
```

## HOW TO ASK FOR CODE AS A BEGINNER

Use this exact sequence with any coding model:

1. Ask for the folder structure
2. Ask for the database models
3. Ask for schemas
4. Ask for one engine service
5. Ask for one router
6. Ask for one test file
7. Run it
8. Paste the error back and ask for a fix

Do **not** ask for:
- full backend + frontend in one prompt
- all 7 engines in one response
- “complete production app” without file boundaries

Do ask for:
- exact filenames
- exact dependencies
- exact `.env` variables
- exact terminal commands
- sample request/response payloads
- tests for the module being generated

Recommended repo layout for implementation:

```text
eureka-platform/
  apps/
     web/                          # Next.js frontend
  services/
     api/
        app/
          main.py
          core/
          routers/
          schemas/
          db/
          services/
             engines/
             workflows/
          prompts/
  infra/
     docker/
     k8s/
     github-actions/
  docs/
```

## ACCOUNTS TO CREATE BEFORE YOU BUILD

| Service | Why You Need It | Minimum Recommendation |
|---------|-----------------|------------------------|
| GitHub | Source control, PR workflow, Actions CI/CD | 1 org or repo owner account |
| Anthropic Console | Architecture research workflows (build-time) | API account (GitHub Student Plan covers this) |
| Google AI Studio | Engine 5 Lecture Companion (runtime — free tier) + build-time research | Free account — aistudio.google.com, no billing required |
| Groq Cloud | Engines 1, 2, 3, 4, 6 and Engine 7 fallback (runtime — free tier) | Free account — console.groq.com, no credit card required |
| PostgreSQL host (Neon, Supabase, RDS, Azure PG) | Primary relational database | 1 managed Postgres instance |
| Qdrant Cloud | Vector search for topics, interests, similarity matching | 1 cluster |
| Cloudflare R2 or MinIO | Store lecture uploads, generated assets, cached files | 1 bucket or object store |
| Redis / Upstash | Caching, job state, orchestration, rate limiting | 1 Redis instance |
| Web Push API (optional, later) | Browser push notifications for offline users (via service worker) | No external dependency — uses native Web Push API + VAPID keys |
| Resend / SendGrid | Transactional email notifications | 1 sending domain/account |
| Twilio (optional) | SMS escalation for admin alerts | 1 account if SMS needed |
| Vercel (or your preferred host) | Next.js deployment | 1 project |
| Docker Hub or GHCR | Container registry for backend/frontend images | 1 registry namespace |
| Sentry | Error monitoring across web and backend | 1 org/project |
| Domain/DNS provider | App domain, API subdomain, auth redirects | 1 domain |

If you want the smallest viable production stack, create these first:
- GitHub
- Groq Cloud (free — console.groq.com)
- Google AI Studio (free — aistudio.google.com)
- HuggingFace (free — for NLLB-200 and Whisper model downloads)
- Managed PostgreSQL
- Qdrant Cloud
- MinIO (self-hosted)
- Redis / Upstash

---

## WEB APP DESIGN PHILOSOPHY

Project Eureka's web app must feel like a **Knowledge Observatory** — a sophisticated, immersive instrument that reveals hidden patterns in a student's learning journey. The visual design communicates three things instantly:

1. **Intelligence** — This platform is built by people who understand education deeply
2. **Warmth** — Despite the AI backbone, every interaction feels human and encouraging
3. **Clarity** — Complex data becomes intuitive through thoughtful visualization

### Design Principles

| Principle | What It Means | How It Shows |
|-----------|---------------|--------------|
| Progressive Disclosure | Don't overwhelm — reveal complexity gradually | Expandable cards, tabbed views, "Show more" patterns |
| Data as Story | Numbers alone don't motivate — context and narrative do | Animated charts with annotations, progress narratives |
| Spatial Consistency | Same types of info always appear in the same screen zones | Fixed navigation, consistent card layouts, predictable modes |
| Purposeful Motion | Every animation serves a function — guiding attention or confirming action | Spring-physics transitions, skeleton loaders, success celebrations |
| Inclusive Beauty | Beautiful AND accessible — never sacrifice one for the other | WCAG AA contrast, semantic HTML, keyboard nav, screen reader labels |
| Contextual Intelligence | The UI adapts to the student's specialization and learning state | Specialization accent colors, personalized dashboards, contextual tips |

---

## VISUAL DESIGN SYSTEM

### Color Palette

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ PRIMARY (Trust & Intelligence)                                │
│ Sapphire-50:  #eff6ff    Sapphire-500: #3b82f6               │
│ Sapphire-100: #dbeafe    Sapphire-600: #2563eb               │
│ Sapphire-900: #1e3a5f    Sapphire-950: #0f172a (dark base)   │
│                                                               │
│ SECONDARY (Growth & Discovery)                                │
│ Emerald-50:  #ecfdf5     Emerald-500: #10b981                │
│ Emerald-700: #047857                                          │
│                                                               │
│ ACCENT (Achievement & Warmth)                                 │
│ Amber-50:   #fffbeb      Amber-500:  #f59e0b                 │
│ Amber-700:  #b45309      Rose-500:   #f43f5e (alerts only)   │
│                                                               │
│ SURFACES                                                      │
│ Light mode:  #ffffff → #f8fafc → #f1f5f9                     │
│ Dark mode:   #0f172a → #1e293b → #334155                     │
│                                                               │
│ SPECIALIZATION ACCENT COLORS (contextual UI tinting)          │
│ Embedded Systems:     Teal     #0d9488                        │
│ VLSI Design:          Violet   #7c3aed                        │
│ Signal Processing:    Cyan     #06b6d4                        │
│ IoT & Sensors:        Orange   #ea580c                        │
│ Communication Sys:    Indigo   #4f46e5                        │
│ RF Engineering:       Pink     #db2777                        │
│ Computer Science:     Sky      #0284c7                        │
│ Mechanical:           Stone    #78716c                        │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

When a student's specialization is known, their dashboard subtly tints with the specialization accent color — header gradient, progress bars, and active nav indicators shift to match. This creates a personalized feel without custom theme work.

### Typography Scale

| Level | Font | Size | Weight | Use |
|-------|------|------|--------|-----|
| Display | Inter | 48px / 3rem | 800 | Hero headings, welcome messages |
| H1 | Inter | 36px / 2.25rem | 700 | Page titles |
| H2 | Inter | 24px / 1.5rem | 600 | Section headers |
| H3 | Inter | 20px / 1.25rem | 600 | Card titles |
| Body | Inter | 16px / 1rem | 400 | Paragraphs, descriptions |
| Small | Inter | 14px / 0.875rem | 400 | Captions, metadata, timestamps |
| Code | JetBrains Mono | 14px / 0.875rem | 400 | Code snippets, formulas, IDs |

### Motion & Animation System

| Animation Type | Library | Duration | Easing | Use Case |
|----------------|---------|----------|--------|----------|
| Page transitions | Framer Motion | 300-400ms | ease-out | Route changes, modal open/close |
| Micro-interactions | Framer Motion | 150-250ms | spring (stiffness: 300, damping: 25) | Button hover, card expansion, toggle |
| Data loading | CSS + Tailwind | 1.5s loop | ease-in-out | Skeleton shimmer, progress pulse |
| Chart animations | Recharts + Framer Motion | 800ms | ease-out | Chart mount, data update |
| Celebrations | canvas-confetti | 2-3s | physics-based | Achievement unlock, milestone |
| AI processing | CSS keyframes | Infinite until done | ease-in-out | Pulsing dots, typing indicator, progress ring |
| Drag/reorder | dnd-kit + Framer | Real-time | spring | Dashboard widget reorder |
| Scroll reveals | Framer Motion (whileInView) | 400ms | ease-out | Cards/sections animate in on scroll |

### Component Design Language

| Component | Style | Key Detail |
|-----------|-------|------------|
| Cards | rounded-xl (16px), shadow-md, white/slate-900 surface, 1px border | Hover lift (translateY -2px) + shadow-lg |
| Primary buttons | Sapphire-600 bg, white text, rounded-lg, px-6 py-3 | Scale 1.02 on hover, ring-2 on focus |
| Secondary buttons | White bg, sapphire-600 text, border sapphire-300 | Sapphire-50 bg on hover |
| Inputs | rounded-lg, border-slate-300, focus:ring-2 sapphire-500 | Floating labels animated |
| Navigation | Fixed left sidebar (desktop), bottom bar (narrow viewports) | Active: accent color + left border |
| Modals | Backdrop blur(8px) + overlay, spring animation | Focus trap, Escape, aria-modal |
| Toasts | Bottom-right, slide-in, auto-dismiss 5s | Colored left border by severity |
| Progress bars | rounded-full, gradient fill (sapphire→emerald) | Animated fill with counter |
| Badges/Tags | rounded-full, soft colored bg | Pulse on new items |
| Tables | Alternating rows, sticky header, sortable | Smooth sort animation |
| Charts | Rounded bars, gradient fills, hover tooltips | Mount animation, responsive |
| Empty states | Centered illustration + message + CTA | Custom SVG illustrations |
| Skeleton loaders | Shimmer gradient over rounded shapes | Match exact loaded layout |

### Glass Effect (Feature Cards & Overlays)

For premium surfaces (Discovery results, Achievement modals, AI processing overlay):

```css
backdrop-filter: blur(12px);
background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.75); /* light mode */
background: rgba(15, 23, 42, 0.75);   /* dark mode */
border: 1px solid rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.18);
box-shadow: 0 8px 32px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.08);
```

## FRONTEND TECH STACK

| Technology | Purpose | Why |
|------------|---------|-----|
| **Next.js 15** (App Router) | Framework | Server Components for perf, SSR for SEO, API routes for BFF |
| **Tailwind CSS 4** | Utility CSS | Fast iteration, consistent spacing, responsive-first, dark mode |
| **shadcn/ui** | Component library | Accessible Radix primitives, full customization, copy-paste ownership |
| **Framer Motion** | Animation | Declarative, layout animations, spring physics, AnimatePresence |
| **Recharts** | Data visualization | React-native, responsive, composable, animation-friendly |
| **TanStack Query v5** | Server state | Smart caching, optimistic updates, background refetch, retry |
| **Zustand** | Client state | Minimal API, devtools, persist middleware for user prefs/theme |
| **Socket.IO client** | Real-time | Bi-directional events for live notifications and AI progress |
| **next-themes** | Theme management | Dark/light/system with zero-flash on load |
| **canvas-confetti** | Celebrations | Lightweight particle effects for achievements |
| **react-hot-toast** | Notifications | Beautiful toasts with promise-aware display |
| **dnd-kit** | Drag and drop | Accessible, keyboard-friendly DnD for dashboard customization |
| **date-fns** | Date handling | Tree-shakeable, locale-aware date formatting |
| **zod** | Form validation | Type-safe validation matching backend Pydantic schemas |
| **Lucide React** | Icons | Consistent, tree-shakeable icon set |
| **nuqs** | URL state | Type-safe search params for filters, tabs, pagination |

### Frontend Folder Architecture

```
apps/web/
  app/
    (auth)/                    # Auth group (no sidebar)
      login/page.tsx
      register/page.tsx
      sso/callback/page.tsx
    (dashboard)/               # Main app shell (sidebar + header)
      layout.tsx               # Shell with sidebar nav + header
      student/
        page.tsx               # Student home dashboard
        discovery/
          page.tsx             # Discovery lab interface
          results/page.tsx     # Results + radar chart
        roadmap/page.tsx       # Interactive timeline
        courses/
          page.tsx             # Course list + context cards
          [courseId]/page.tsx   # Course detail
        assessments/
          page.tsx             # Assessment list + history
          [assessmentId]/page.tsx  # Timed quiz
          results/[id]/page.tsx    # Score reveal
        lectures/
          page.tsx             # Lecture companions
          [lectureId]/page.tsx # Full companion viewer
        profile/page.tsx       # Profile + settings
        achievements/page.tsx  # Badges, streaks, XP
      professor/
        page.tsx               # Professor dashboard
        upload/page.tsx        # Drag-drop upload + status
        analytics/
          page.tsx             # Class performance
          [courseId]/page.tsx   # Per-course heatmap
        courses/page.tsx       # Course management
        companions/page.tsx    # Review companions
        feedback/page.tsx      # Anonymized feedback
      admin/
        page.tsx               # Command center
        substitutes/page.tsx   # Substitute routing
        feedback/page.tsx      # Feedback processing
        procurement/page.tsx   # Hardware evaluation
        analytics/page.tsx     # System analytics
        users/page.tsx         # User management
    api/                       # BFF proxy to FastAPI
  components/
    ui/                        # shadcn/ui base (Button, Card, Input...)
    charts/                    # Recharts wrappers
    engine-views/              # Engine-specific display components
      DiscoveryResults.tsx     # Radar chart + recommendation cards
      RoadmapTimeline.tsx      # Interactive semester timeline
      ContextCardView.tsx      # Expandable card with tabs
      AssessmentPlayer.tsx     # Timed quiz with progress bar
      LectureCompanion.tsx     # Companion viewer with tabs
      AdminSubstitutePanel.tsx # Ranking + approval panel
      FeedbackDashboard.tsx    # Category charts + alerts
      TranslatedContent.tsx    # Language-aware renderer
    layouts/
      DashboardShell.tsx       # Sidebar + header + content
      Sidebar.tsx              # Collapsible, role-based nav
      Header.tsx               # Search, bell, user menu, theme
    shared/
      SkeletonCard.tsx         # Shimmer placeholder
      EmptyState.tsx           # Illustrated empty with CTA
      AIProcessingOverlay.tsx  # Glass panel + pulse animation
      ConfettiCelebration.tsx  # Achievement celebration
      NotificationCenter.tsx   # Slide-out notification panel
      GlobalSearch.tsx         # Cmd+K spotlight search
      OnboardingTour.tsx       # Step-by-step walkthrough
  hooks/
    useAuth.ts
    useEngine.ts               # Engine API call + loading/error
    useRealtime.ts             # Socket.IO subscription
    useTheme.ts                # Theme + accent management
    useConfetti.ts             # Celebration trigger
  lib/
    api-client.ts              # Typed fetch wrapper
    constants.ts               # Colors, routes, roles
    utils.ts                   # Formatting helpers
  stores/
    user-store.ts              # User profile, role, prefs
    notification-store.ts      # Notification queue
    dashboard-store.ts         # Widget layout state
  styles/
    globals.css                # Tailwind + custom utilities
    theme.ts                   # Theme extension
```

### Professor Experience

```
1. DASHBOARD — Quick stats, recent uploads with status, class heatmap
2. UPLOAD — Drag-drop zone, progress bars, AI processing indicator,
   inline companion preview, approve/edit flow
3. ANALYTICS — Score distribution, trend charts, topic mastery heatmap,
   at-risk student alerts with action buttons
```

### Admin Experience

```
1. COMMAND CENTER — Action summary badges (substitutes/feedback/procurement),
   substitute panel with AI score + approve/override, feedback donut chart,
   procurement queue with cost-benefit + AI decision
```

## GAMIFICATION & ENGAGEMENT LAYER

| Feature | Trigger | Visual |
|---------|---------|--------|
| **Learning Streak** | Any daily learning activity | 🔥 flame icon + day count, grows with streak |
| **XP System** | Assessment +50, card +10, streak +25, perfect +100 | Animated counter in header, level-up celebrate |
| **Achievement Badges** | Milestones (first quiz, 7-day streak, etc.) | Glass badge card + confetti burst on unlock |
| **Progress Rings** | Real-time on dashboard | Animated SVG ring with percentage |
| **Weekly Goal** | Self-set in profile | Progress bar with encouraging copy |
| **Concept Mastery Map** | Updated after assessments | Node graph, color-coded mastery levels |

---

## REAL-TIME ARCHITECTURE

| Event | Transport | Source → Consumer |
|-------|-----------|-------------------|
| Lecture companion ready | SSE | Backend → Student dashboard (toast) |
| Assessment available | SSE | Backend → Student bell notification |
| AI processing progress | WebSocket | Backend → Upload page (live ring) |
| Substitute assigned | SSE | Backend → Admin + substitute + students |
| Translation ready | SSE | Backend → Student (content available) |
| Critical feedback | WebSocket | Backend → Admin (immediate alert) |
| Score published | SSE | Backend → Student + Professor analytics |
| Achievement unlocked | WebSocket | Backend → Student (confetti + modal) |

**Implementation:** FastAPI `StreamingResponse` for SSE at `/api/v1/events/stream`, Socket.IO for bidirectional, TanStack Query poll for non-critical, Zustand for client notification queue.

---

## ACCESSIBILITY REQUIREMENTS (WCAG 2.1 AA)

| Requirement | Implementation |
|-------------|---------------|
| Color contrast | 4.5:1 normal text, 3:1 large text |
| Keyboard nav | All elements focusable, logical tab order, Escape closes modals |
| Screen readers | ARIA labels, `aria-live` regions, meaningful alt text |
| Reduced motion | `prefers-reduced-motion` disables all animations |
| Focus indicators | ring-2 ring-sapphire-500 ring-offset-2 |
| Form a11y | Labels linked to inputs, `aria-describedby` for errors |
| Skip links | "Skip to main content" link (hidden until focused) |
| Semantic HTML | h1→h2→h3 hierarchy, landmarks, lists |
| Color independence | Info never by color alone — paired with icons/labels |
| Zoom support | Text scales to 200% without layout break |

---

## PERFORMANCE REQUIREMENTS

| Metric | Target | Strategy |
|--------|--------|----------|
| First Contentful Paint | < 1.5s | SSR + font preload |
| Largest Contentful Paint | < 2.5s | next/image + WebP, lazy loading |
| Cumulative Layout Shift | < 0.1 | Skeleton loaders, fixed dimensions |
| Time to Interactive | < 3.5s | Code splitting, dynamic imports |
| Initial bundle | < 150KB gzip | Tree shaking, Server Components default |
| API (non-AI) | < 500ms | Redis cache, PG indexes, CDN |
| AI engine | < 30s with progress | Background jobs, SSE status, optimistic UI |

---

## ERROR STATE UX SPECIFICATIONS

Every screen must handle three failure modes gracefully. Never show raw error messages or blank screens.

### Global Error States

| Error Type | UI Treatment | User Action |
|------------|-------------|-------------|
| **Network offline** | Top banner: "You're offline. Changes will sync when you reconnect." (amber, `bg-amber-500/10`) | Auto-dismiss on reconnect, pulse animation |
| **API 500 / Unknown** | Centered card with illustration + "Something went wrong" + error ID | "Try Again" button (retry with exponential backoff, max 3) + "Report Issue" link |
| **API 401 / Session expired** | Modal overlay: "Your session has expired" | "Sign In Again" button → redirect to `/auth/login` preserving current route |
| **API 403 / Forbidden** | Inline warning: "You don't have permission to view this" | "Go to Dashboard" link |
| **API 429 / Rate limit** | Toast: "Too many requests. Please wait a moment." | Auto-retry after `Retry-After` header value |

### AI Engine Error States

| Scenario | UI Treatment |
|----------|-------------|
| **AI taking > 10s** | Show skeleton + progress bar + "Still thinking..." message + estimated time |
| **AI taking > 30s** | Switch to "This is taking longer than usual. We'll notify you when ready." + option to continue browsing |
| **AI provider down** | Card: "Our AI service is temporarily unavailable. Your request has been queued." + auto-retry |
| **AI response malformed** | Silent retry (1x). On second failure: "We couldn't generate your [result]. Please try again." |

### Form / Input Error States

| Pattern | Implementation |
|---------|---------------|
| **Inline validation** | Red border (`ring-red-500/50`) + error text below field + shake animation (150ms) |
| **Form submission failure** | Toast (red, top-right): specific error message. Form fields retain entered values. |
| **File upload failure** | Progress bar turns red + "Upload failed: [reason]" + "Retry" button |

### Empty States (Zero Data)

| Screen | Empty State |
|--------|-------------|
| **Student — no assessments yet** | Illustration + "No assessments yet. Your first quiz will appear after your next lecture." |
| **Professor — no courses** | Illustration + "Add your first course to get started" + CTA button |
| **Admin — no feedback** | Illustration + "No feedback submitted yet. Feedback will appear here as students and professors respond." |
| **Search — no results** | "No results for '[query]'. Try broadening your search." |

> **Implementation:** Create a shared `<ErrorBoundary>` component wrapping each page, a `<RetryableQuery>` wrapper around TanStack Query hooks, and an `<EmptyState icon={} title="" description="" action={}>` component.

---

## SEQUENTIAL IMPLEMENTATION STRATEGY

Build the app in this order and place code in these locations:

1. **Create the monorepo skeleton**
    - `apps/web`
    - `services/api/app`
    - `infra/`

2. **Build the backend foundation first**
    - Database models in `services/api/app/db/models/`
    - Pydantic schemas in `services/api/app/schemas/`
    - Common config in `services/api/app/core/config.py`
    - FastAPI bootstrap in `services/api/app/main.py`

3. **Create the prompt library**
    - Store the 7 engine prompts as versioned files in `services/api/app/prompts/`
    - Suggested filenames:
      - `discovery_system_prompt.md`
      - `context_system_prompt.md`
      - `journey_system_prompt.md`
      - `evaluation_system_prompt.md`
      - `companion_system_prompt.md`
      - `admin_system_prompt.md`
      - `translation_system_prompt.md`

    **Prompt Versioning Strategy:**
    - Each prompt file uses YAML frontmatter for metadata:
      ```
      ---
      engine: discovery
      version: "1.0.0"
      model: claude-opus-4.6
      last_modified: 2026-08-15
      author: eureka-team
      changelog:
        - "1.0.0: Initial production prompt"
      ---
      [prompt body]
      ```
    - Version format: `MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH` (major = behavioral change, minor = quality improvement, patch = typo/formatting)
    - The prompt loader (`services/api/app/core/prompt_loader.py`) reads the frontmatter and logs `{engine, version, model}` on every AI call for auditability
    - A/B testing: support loading `discovery_system_prompt_v2.md` alongside `v1` via a feature flag in Redis
    - All prompt changes must be committed to git — the prompt file IS the source of truth (no database-stored prompts)

4. **Implement one engine at a time in backend services**
    - Engine services in `services/api/app/services/engines/`
    - API routes in `services/api/app/routers/`
    - Keep prompt loading inside the matching engine service, not in the route handler

5. **Wire orchestration only after individual engines work**
    - Workflow logic in `services/api/app/services/workflows/orchestrator.py`
    - Shared handoff schemas in `services/api/app/schemas/handoffs.py`
    - Background jobs and retries in `services/api/app/services/jobs/`

6. **Then build the complete web app** (design system, all screens, gamification, real-time events)
    - Student / professor / admin web flows in `apps/web`
    - Keep the initial UI intentionally minimal: forms, tables, upload screens, and result viewers
    - The web app must call backend APIs only; it must never call model providers directly

7. **Finish with infrastructure and deployment**
    - Docker assets in `infra/docker/`
    - Kubernetes manifests in `infra/k8s/`
    - CI/CD in `.github/workflows/`

## BEGINNER EXECUTION CHECKLIST

Before asking any AI to generate code, make sure you have:

- one empty GitHub repository
- one local project folder
- one Python environment for backend work
- one Node.js install for web work
- API keys ready for Groq (free) and Google AI Studio (free) if you are testing model calls

When generating code, ask for output in this order:

1. `requirements.txt` or `pyproject.toml`
2. backend app bootstrap
3. database models
4. schemas
5. service layer
6. router layer
7. tests
8. design system and component library setup
9. web app screens (student → professor → admin)
10. gamification, notifications, real-time
11. orchestration, testing, deployment

## MASTER BUILDER PROMPT

Use this prompt with **GPT-5.4** or **GPT-5.2 Codex** whenever you want code generated from this strategy:

```text
I am a beginner building Project Eureka step by step. Do not give me the whole platform at once.

I want you to generate only the code for the current step, with complete file contents, exact filenames, required dependencies, .env variables, terminal commands, and test instructions.

Tech stack:
- Backend: FastAPI + PostgreSQL + Qdrant + Redis
- Web: Next.js 15 (App Router) + Tailwind CSS 4 + shadcn/ui + Framer Motion + Recharts
- Storage: MinIO or Cloudflare R2
- AI providers: Groq free tier (Engines 1–4, 6), Google AI Studio free tier (Engine 5), NLLB-200 self-hosted (Engine 7)
- State: TanStack Query v5 (server) + Zustand (client)
- Real-time: Socket.IO + Server-Sent Events
- Theme: next-themes (dark/light/system)

Rules:
- Generate production-grade code, but beginner-friendly structure
- Keep modules isolated and integration-ready
- Explain where each file should be placed
- Include a short “what this file does” note before each file
- Add a short validation checklist at the end
- If there are design choices, choose the simplest production-safe option

Current step:
[PASTE ONE TASK FROM THIS STRATEGY HERE]
```

---

## ENGINE-BY-ENGINE BUILD ORDER

Use this sequence to reduce integration risk:

1. Phase 1 foundation (DB, FastAPI scaffold, auth, CI/CD)
2. Engine 1 Discovery (student profiling)
3. Engine 3 Learning Journey (roadmap generation)
4. Engine 2 Curriculum Context (context cards)
5. Engine 5 Lecture Companion (multimodal processing)
6. Engine 4 Evaluation (micro-assessments)
7. Engine 7 Translation (multilingual delivery)
8. Engine 6 Admin Assistant (operational workflows)
9. Web app design system + component library
10. Web app auth + dashboard shell + student screens
11. Web app professor screens + admin screens
12. Gamification, real-time, notifications
13. Orchestration layer wiring
14. End-to-end testing + security audit
15. Performance optimization + deployment

---

## PHASE 1: Foundation & Core Infrastructure
**Goal:** Establish the technical backbone — database schemas, authentication, API gateway, and base deployment pipeline.

| Task ID | Task Name | Description | Assigned Model | Reason |
|---------|-----------|-------------|----------------|--------|
| P1-T1 | Database Schema Design | Design PostgreSQL schemas for students, professors, admins, courses, labs, specializations, assessments, and feedback. Include Qdrant vector collection schemas for RAG embeddings. | GPT-5.1 Codex Max | Complex systems architecture code requiring full-stack DB expertise with production-grade indexing and migration strategy. |
| P1-T2 | FastAPI Backend Scaffold | Generate the complete FastAPI project structure with routers, middleware (auth, CORS, rate-limiting), dependency injection, and Pydantic models for all 7 engines. | GPT-5.2 Codex | Advanced code synthesis with strong FastAPI integration patterns and structured decision-making. |
| P1-T3 | Authentication & RBAC System | Implement JWT-based auth with role-based access control for Student, Professor, and Administrator roles, including OAuth2 integration for university SSO. | GPT-5.1 Codex | Production code expertise for security-critical full-stack authentication flows. |
| P1-T4 | Dev Infrastructure Scaffold | Create Docker Compose for local development (FastAPI + PostgreSQL + Qdrant + Redis + MinIO), basic GitHub Actions CI with linting/type-check gates, and MinIO/R2 storage configuration. **Scope: local dev only — no K8s, no production images.** | GPT-5.1 Codex Mini | Utility scripts and boilerplate infrastructure code — well within lightweight operational scope. |

---

## PHASE 2: Discovery & Profiling Engines
**Goal:** Build Engine 1 (Discovery) and Engine 3 (Personalized Learning Journey) — the student onboarding and roadmap generation system.

| Task ID | Task Name | Description | Assigned Model | Reason |
|---------|-----------|-------------|----------------|--------|
| P2-T1 | Discovery Engine AI Agent Prompt | Craft the production system prompt for the Discovery Engine that profiles students during Week 1 lab exploration and recommends specializations. | Claude Opus 4.6 | Extended thinking, nuanced human intent interpretation, and pedagogical reasoning for complex student profiling. |
| P2-T2 | Student Interest Tracking Pipeline | Build the event ingestion pipeline that captures behavioral signals (time spent, clicks, quiz scores) from lab exploration UI and stores them in PostgreSQL + Qdrant. | GPT-5.2 Codex | Backend logic with structured decision-making for real-time event processing and FastAPI integration. |
| P2-T3 | Personalized Learning Journey Prompt | Craft the production system prompt for the Learning Journey Engine that generates semester-by-semester academic roadmaps. | Claude Opus 4.6 | Long-horizon planning with structured output — requires deep reasoning across course catalogs, industry data, and student profiles. |
| P2-T4 | Roadmap Generation API Endpoint | Implement the FastAPI endpoint that orchestrates Groq Llama 3.3 70B calls for roadmap generation, including caching, retry logic, and structured JSON response parsing. | GPT-5.1 Codex | Production code for API orchestration with robust error handling and model integration. |

---

## PHASE 3: Curriculum Intelligence & Context Engine
**Goal:** Build Engine 2 (Curriculum Context Engine) — the system that eliminates the Context Vacuum by generating real-world Context Cards for every syllabus topic.

| Task ID | Task Name | Description | Assigned Model | Reason |
|---------|-----------|-------------|----------------|--------|
| P3-T1 | Curriculum Context Engine Prompt | Craft the production system prompt that generates Context Cards linking syllabus topics to industry deployments, campus labs, hiring data, and worked examples. | Claude Opus 4.6 | Deep domain knowledge and cross-domain reasoning required to connect academic concepts to specific deployed industrial systems. |
| P3-T2 | Syllabus Ingestion & Topic Extraction | Build the RAG pipeline that ingests university syllabi (PDF/DOCX), extracts individual topics, and stores embeddings in Qdrant for retrieval. | Gemini 3.1 Pro | Long-context data analysis and document processing — excels at parsing large academic documents. |
| P3-T3 | Context Card Storage & Retrieval API | Implement CRUD endpoints for Context Cards with vector similarity search for related topics and caching layer for frequently accessed cards. | GPT-5.1 Codex | Production full-stack development for API endpoints with vector DB integration. |
| P3-T4 | Lab Inventory Database & Matching | Build the university lab inventory system and implement fuzzy matching between syllabus topics and available lab experiments. | Claude Sonnet 4.6 | Balanced structured output with accurate matching logic — ideal for classification and mapping tasks. |

---

## PHASE 4: Assessment & Lecture Companion
**Goal:** Build Engine 4 (Continuous Understanding Evaluation) and Engine 5 (AI Lecture Companion) — the daily learning loop engines.

| Task ID | Task Name | Description | Assigned Model | Reason |
|---------|-----------|-------------|----------------|--------|
| P4-T1 | Continuous Evaluation Engine Prompt | Craft the production system prompt that generates adaptive micro-assessments, scores responses, identifies gaps, and adjusts difficulty. | Claude Sonnet 4.6 | Fast, accurate structured output — optimal for high-volume per-student evaluation with calibrated question generation. |
| P4-T2 | AI Lecture Companion Prompt | Craft the production system prompt that processes multimodal lecture materials and generates structured companion documents with summaries, applications, and catch-up packages. | Gemini 3.1 Pro | Native multimodal processing (PDF + slides + audio + text) with 1M context window for full lecture history. |
| P4-T3 | Lecture Upload & Processing Pipeline | Build the file upload system (MinIO/R2) with automatic format detection, transcription (audio→text), and structured extraction from slides/PDFs. | GPT-5.2 Codex | Backend pipeline logic with multi-format file handling and FastAPI integration. |
| P4-T4 | Student Performance Tracking & Analytics | Implement the performance tracking system that stores micro-test results, computes trend analytics, and feeds gap analysis back to Engine 3 for roadmap adjustment. | GPT-5.1 Codex | Production database operations with analytics queries and API endpoint development. |
| P4-T5 | Adaptive Difficulty Algorithm | Design and implement the spaced-repetition and adaptive difficulty algorithm that calibrates question difficulty based on student performance history. | Claude Opus 4.6 | Deep analysis and research synthesis for designing pedagogically sound adaptive algorithms. |

## PHASE 5: Administration & Translation
**Goal:** Build Engine 6 (Academic Administration Assistant) and Engine 7 (Global Knowledge Translation Engine) — the operational backbone.

| Task ID | Task Name | Description | Assigned Model | Reason |
|---------|-----------|-------------|----------------|--------|
| P5-T1 | Administration Assistant Prompt | Craft the production system prompt for substitute routing, feedback categorization, and hardware procurement evaluation. | GPT-5.2 Codex | Backend logic with structured decision trees — 91% accuracy on complex routing and procurement justification tasks. |
| P5-T2 | Global Translation Engine Prompt | Craft the production system prompt for multilingual content translation preserving technical accuracy across 100+ languages. | Gemini 3.1 Pro | Best-in-class multilingual accuracy with native support for 100+ languages and translation + summarization. |
| P5-T3 | Substitute Routing Algorithm | Implement the decision tree that matches absent professors with available substitutes based on expertise, schedule, and student feedback ratings. | GPT-5.2 Codex | Structured decision-making with explicit routing logic and JSON output for API integration. |
| P5-T4 | Anonymous Feedback Pipeline | Build the secure anonymous feedback submission system with NLP categorization (urgency, type, department) and admin dashboard API. | facebook/bart-large-mnli (self-hosted) | Zero-shot classification via HuggingFace pipeline — free, CPU-efficient, no API key needed for categorizing feedback by type and urgency. |
| P5-T5 | NLLB-200 Integration | Implement the translation pipeline using NLLB-200 distilled-600M (primary, self-hosted) with Groq Llama 3.3 70B fallback for complex or high-priority content. | GPT-5.1 Codex Mini | Utility integration scripts and boilerplate code for model pipeline wiring. |

## PHASE 6: Web App — Design System, Screens & Interactions
**Goal:** Build the complete Next.js web app with the full design system, all role-based screens, gamification, real-time events, and polished user experience. This is not a "minimal shell" — it is the production web app.

| Task ID | Task Name | Description | Assigned Model | Reason |
|---------|-----------|-------------|----------------|--------|
| P6-T1 | Design System & Component Library | Set up Tailwind CSS 4, shadcn/ui, Framer Motion, theme provider (dark/light/system), color tokens (sapphire/emerald/amber/specialization accents), typography scale (Inter + JetBrains Mono), and all reusable components: cards, buttons, inputs, modals, toasts, skeletons, empty states, progress rings, glass-effect panels. | GPT-5.4 | Strongest frontend generation; this task requires deep Tailwind + Radix + Framer Motion integration with theme-aware styling. |
| P6-T2 | Auth Pages & Dashboard Shell | Build login page (email + SSO), registration with role selection, OAuth2 callback handler, collapsible sidebar with role-based nav items, header with global search (Cmd+K), notification bell, user menu, theme toggle, and the responsive dashboard layout wrapping all role pages. | GPT-5.4 | Full-stack web integration — auth flow + persistent layout + state management in one cohesive shell. |
| P6-T3 | Student Screens (Discovery → Roadmap → Dashboard) | Build the onboarding welcome screen with particle background, Discovery Lab interactive station cards, Discovery Results page with animated radar chart and evidence cards, interactive semester roadmap timeline (click-to-expand, workload gauges, milestone icons), daily learning dashboard with today's items, semester progress ring, context card previews, and next achievement tracker. | GPT-5.4 | Most complex set of interactive pages — requires Framer Motion animations, Recharts radar/bar charts, and TanStack Query data orchestration. |
| P6-T4 | Student Screens (Assessments + Lectures + Profile) | Build the timed assessment player (progress bar, timer, code-highlighted questions, MCQ/open-ended, animated score reveal, gap analysis radar), lecture companion viewer (tabbed: summary/concepts/applications/lab/catch-up, KaTeX formula rendering, language switcher), and student profile with achievements, badges, streak history, XP, and language preference. | GPT-5.4 | Rich interactive components — timed quiz UX, math rendering, multi-tab content viewer, gamification UI. |
| P6-T5 | Professor Screens (Upload + Analytics + Review) | Build drag-and-drop lecture upload with multi-file progress bars, AI processing overlay with live progress ring (Socket.IO), inline companion preview with approve/edit flow, class analytics dashboard with score distribution chart, topic mastery heatmap, trend-over-time line chart, at-risk student alerts with action buttons. | GPT-5.4 | Data-heavy dashboards requiring Recharts integration, Socket.IO real-time, and complex upload flow. |
| P6-T6 | Admin Screens (Command Center + Substitutes + Feedback + Procurement) | Build admin command center with action summary badges, substitute panel with AI-scored ranking table and approve/override buttons, feedback processing dashboard with category donut chart, urgency filters, critical alert banners, and hardware procurement queue with cost-benefit display and AI recommendation cards. | GPT-5.4 | Operational dashboards with dense data tables, interactive charts, and approval workflows. |
| P6-T7 | Gamification, Notifications & Real-time | Implement XP system (counters, level-up animations), achievement badge system (unlock logic + confetti celebrations via canvas-confetti), learning streak tracker, notification center (slide-out panel with categorized items), SSE subscription hook for live updates, Socket.IO integration for AI processing status, and onboarding tour (step-by-step guided walkthrough for first-time users). | GPT-5.4 | Animation-heavy, event-driven features requiring Framer Motion, Socket.IO, and persistent Zustand state. |
| P6-T8 | API Client, State Management & Error Handling | Build typed API client wrapping all FastAPI endpoints, TanStack Query hooks for all engine calls with optimistic updates, Zustand stores (user, notifications, dashboard layout), global error boundary with friendly error pages, loading states with skeleton components, and offline-aware dashboard shell. | GPT-5.2 Codex | Backend-adjacent API wiring — requires exact typing alignment with FastAPI schemas and robust error handling. |

---

## PHASE 7: Orchestration, Hardening & Deployment
**Goal:** Wire all 7 engines through the orchestration layer, perform end-to-end integration testing, security audit, performance optimization, and ship to production.

| Task ID | Task Name | Description | Assigned Model | Reason |
|---------|-----------|-------------|----------------|--------|
| P7-T1 | Orchestration Layer & Workflow Engine | Implement the central orchestration service that routes requests to the correct engine, manages handoffs between engines, maintains workflow state in Redis, handles retries, dead-letter queues, and publishes SSE events on workflow completion. | Claude Opus 4.6 | Highest-complexity architectural task — requires understanding all 7 engine interactions and fault-tolerant handoff logic. |
| P7-T2 | End-to-End Integration Testing | Write integration tests covering all 3 workflows (A: Onboarding, B: Daily Loop, C: Admin), test engine chaining with mock AI responses, verify data flows from Engine 1 → 3 → 2 → 7, test real-time events, and validate all role-based API authorization. | GPT-5.2 Codex | Comprehensive test writing requiring understanding of all engine schemas and workflow sequences. |
| P7-T3 | Load Testing & Performance Optimization | Design and execute load tests simulating 10,000 concurrent students, optimize database queries, implement Redis caching strategies, tune API response times, optimize Next.js bundle and implement code splitting for heavy components. | Gemini 3.1 Pro | Long-context data analysis for performance profiling and optimization research across full stack. |
| P7-T4 | Security Audit & Compliance | Conduct security review of all 7 engine endpoints + auth system + file upload, implement rate limiting, input sanitization, GDPR compliance for student data, OWASP Top 10 verification, and CSP headers for the web app. | Claude Sonnet 4.6 | Security-focused analysis with strong constraint-following for policy evaluation. |
| P7-T5 | Production CI/CD & Deployment | Build production-optimized Docker images for FastAPI and Next.js, create Kubernetes manifests for staging/prod, expand GitHub Actions CI/CD with full test gates (unit + integration + e2e), set up Sentry error monitoring, and configure domain/DNS with SSL. **Scope: production hardening only — extends P1-T4’s dev scaffold.** | GPT-5.1 Codex Mini | Infrastructure scripts and deployment configuration — well within operational scope. |

---

## PHASE DEPENDENCY MAP

```
Phase 1 (Foundation)  ──→ Phase 2 (Discovery + Journey)
                      ──→ Phase 3 (Curriculum Context)

Phase 2 + Phase 3    ──→ Phase 4 (Assessment + Lecture)

Phase 4              ──→ Phase 5 (Admin + Translation)

Phase 1–5 (Backend)  ──→ Phase 6 (Web App Build)

Phase 5 + Phase 6    ──→ Phase 7 (Orchestration + Hardening + Deploy)
```

## ENVIRONMENT VARIABLE MASTER REFERENCE

> All services read from a single `.env` file in development (loaded by Docker Compose).  
> In production, these are injected via Kubernetes Secrets / Vercel Environment Variables.

| Variable | Service | Example | Notes |
|----------|---------|---------|-------|
| `DATABASE_URL` | FastAPI | `postgresql+asyncpg://eureka:pass@localhost:5432/eureka` | AsyncPG connection string |
| `QDRANT_URL` | FastAPI | `http://localhost:6333` | Qdrant vector DB |
| `QDRANT_API_KEY` | FastAPI | `qdrant_secret_key` | Required in production |
| `REDIS_URL` | FastAPI | `redis://localhost:6379/0` | Cache + job queue |
| `MINIO_ENDPOINT` | FastAPI | `localhost:9000` | Object storage (lecture files) |
| `MINIO_ACCESS_KEY` | FastAPI | `minioadmin` | |
| `MINIO_SECRET_KEY` | FastAPI | `minioadmin` | |
| `MINIO_BUCKET` | FastAPI | `eureka-uploads` | Auto-created by init script |
| `GROQ_API_KEY` | FastAPI | `gsk_...` | Engines 1, 2, 3, 4, 6 and Engine 7 fallback (Groq free tier) |
| `GOOGLE_AI_STUDIO_KEY` | FastAPI | `AIza...` | Engine 5 (Gemini 2.0 Flash, Google AI Studio free tier) |
| `NLLB_MODEL_PATH` | FastAPI | `facebook/nllb-200-distilled-600M` | Engine 7 primary translation model (self-hosted) |
| `WHISPER_MODEL_SIZE` | FastAPI | `large-v3` | Engine 5 audio transcription (self-hosted Whisper) |
| `JWT_SECRET_KEY` | FastAPI | `super-secret-256-bit-key` | HMAC-SHA256 signing |
| `JWT_ALGORITHM` | FastAPI | `HS256` | |
| `JWT_EXPIRY_MINUTES` | FastAPI | `60` | Access token lifetime |
| `CORS_ORIGINS` | FastAPI | `http://localhost:3000` | Comma-separated allowed origins |
| `SENTRY_DSN_BACKEND` | FastAPI | `https://...@sentry.io/...` | Error tracking (production) |
| `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` | Next.js | `http://localhost:8000/api/v1` | Backend API base URL |
| `NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL` | Next.js | `ws://localhost:8000/ws` | WebSocket/SSE endpoint |
| `NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN` | Next.js | `https://...@sentry.io/...` | Frontend error tracking |
| `NEXTAUTH_SECRET` | Next.js | `next-auth-secret-key` | Session encryption |
| `NEXTAUTH_URL` | Next.js | `http://localhost:3000` | Canonical app URL |

> **P1-T4** generates the `.env.example` file with all variables listed above.  
> **P7-T5** maps these to Kubernetes Secrets and Vercel env config.
